5	// TestLoopConfigOpts names every durable knob the execute-bead loop
6	// reads via ResolvedConfig. Tests must specify each field explicitly;
7	// there are no zero-value defaults that silently bypass real config.
8	//
9	// See SD-024 / TD-024 §Test config constructors.
10	type TestLoopConfigOpts struct {
11		Assignee                string
12		ReviewMaxRetries        int
13		NoProgressCooldown      time.Duration
14		MaxNoChangesBeforeClose int
15		HeartbeatInterval       time.Duration
16		Harness                 string
17		Model                   string
18		Profile                 string
19		MinTier                 string
20		MaxTier                 string
21		EvidenceCaps            EvidenceCapsConfig
22	}

24	// NewTestConfigForLoop returns a *Config that, when Resolve()d with the
25	// matching CLIOverrides, produces a ResolvedConfig whose loop-relevant
26	// accessors return exactly the values supplied in opts.
27	//
28	// Tests must obtain ResolvedConfig via Resolve on the returned *Config
29	// — there is no shortcut to construct a ResolvedConfig directly. Pure
30	// CLI-override fields (Assignee, Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) are passed
31	// through CLIOverrides at the call site; the constructor stores every
32	// other field on *Config so it round-trips through Resolve.
33	func NewTestConfigForLoop(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) *Config {
34		reviewMaxRetries := opts.ReviewMaxRetries
35		maxNoChanges := opts.MaxNoChangesBeforeClose
36	
37		caps := opts.EvidenceCaps
38	
39		return &Config{
40			Version: "1.0",
41			Agent: &AgentConfig{
42				Harness: opts.Harness,
43				Model:   opts.Model,
44			},
45			Workers: &WorkersConfig{
46				NoProgressCooldown:      opts.NoProgressCooldown.String(),
47				MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: &maxNoChanges,
48				HeartbeatInterval:       opts.HeartbeatInterval.String(),
49			},
50			ReviewMaxRetries: &reviewMaxRetries,
51			EvidenceCaps:     &caps,
52		}
53	}
54	
55	// TestLoopOverrides returns the CLIOverrides that, combined with the
56	// *Config produced by NewTestConfigForLoop(opts), drive a Resolve call
57	// to a ResolvedConfig matching opts. Pure-override fields (Assignee,
58	// Profile, MinTier, MaxTier) have no durable home on *Config; they are
59	// applied at Resolve time only.
60	func TestLoopOverrides(opts TestLoopConfigOpts) CLIOverrides {
61		return CLIOverrides{
62			Assignee: opts.Assignee,
63			Profile:  opts.Profile,
64			MinTier:  opts.MinTier,
65			MaxTier:  opts.MaxTier,
66		}
67	}

499		store, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
500	
501		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
502			Store: store,
503			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
504				if beadID == first.ID {
505					return ExecuteBeadReport{
506						BeadID:    beadID,
507						Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
508						Detail:    "merged cleanly",
509						SessionID: "sess-first",
510						ResultRev: "feedbeef",
511					}, nil
512				}
513				return ExecuteBeadReport{
514					BeadID:      beadID,
515					Status:      ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed,
516					Detail:      "agent execution failed",
517					PreserveRef: "refs/ddx/iterations/" + beadID + "/attempt-1",
518					ResultRev:   "baadf00d",
519				}, nil
520			}),
521		}
522	
523		var sink bytes.Buffer
524		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
525			Assignee:    "worker",
526			EventSink:   &sink,
527			WorkerID:    "worker-42",
528			ProjectRoot: "/tmp/fake-project",
529			Harness:     "claude",
530			Model:       "claude-3.5-sonnet",
531			SessionID:   "agent-loop-test",
532		})
533		require.NoError(t, err)
534		require.NotNil(t, result)
535		assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Attempts)
536	
537		lines := strings.Split(strings.TrimRight(sink.String(), "\n"), "\n")
538		require.GreaterOrEqual(t, len(lines), 6, "expected loop.start + 2*(bead.claimed+bead.result) + loop.end")
539	
540		parse := func(t *testing.T, line string) (string, map[string]any) {
541			t.Helper()
542			var entry map[string]any
543			require.NoError(t, json.Unmarshal([]byte(line), &entry))
544			typ, _ := entry["type"].(string)
545			data, _ := entry["data"].(map[string]any)
546			// Every entry must carry the envelope fields.
547			assert.Equal(t, "agent-loop-test", entry["session_id"])
548			assert.NotEmpty(t, entry["ts"])
549			return typ, data
550		}
551	
552		// First line must be loop.start with metadata.
553		typ, data := parse(t, lines[0])
554		assert.Equal(t, "loop.start", typ)
555		assert.Equal(t, "worker-42", data["worker_id"])
556		assert.Equal(t, "/tmp/fake-project", data["project_root"])
557		assert.Equal(t, "claude", data["harness"])
558		assert.Equal(t, "claude-3.5-sonnet", data["model"])
559	
560		// Collect the rest by type so ordering between beads isn't load-bearing.
561		byType := map[string][]map[string]any{}
562		for _, line := range lines {
563			typ, data := parse(t, line)
564			byType[typ] = append(byType[typ], data)
565		}
566	
567		require.Len(t, byType["loop.start"], 1)
568		require.Len(t, byType["loop.end"], 1)
569		require.Len(t, byType["bead.claimed"], 2)
570		require.Len(t, byType["bead.result"], 2)
571	
572		claimedIDs := []string{}
573		for _, d := range byType["bead.claimed"] {
574			id, _ := d["bead_id"].(string)
575			claimedIDs = append(claimedIDs, id)
576			assert.NotEmpty(t, d["title"], "bead.claimed should carry the title")
577		}
578		assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{first.ID, second.ID}, claimedIDs)
579	
580		// bead.result must carry status + duration_ms for every attempt, and
581		// success/result_rev for the successful bead.
582		var sawSuccess, sawFailure bool
583		for _, d := range byType["bead.result"] {
584			beadID, _ := d["bead_id"].(string)
585			status, _ := d["status"].(string)
586			_, hasDuration := d["duration_ms"]
587			assert.True(t, hasDuration, "bead.result must include duration_ms")
588			if beadID == first.ID {
589				sawSuccess = true
590				assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess, status)
591				assert.Equal(t, "sess-first", d["session_id"])
592				assert.Equal(t, "feedbeef", d["result_rev"])
593			}
594			if beadID == second.ID {
595				sawFailure = true
596				assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, status)
597				assert.Equal(t, "agent execution failed", d["detail"])
598			}
599		}
600		assert.True(t, sawSuccess, "bead.result missing for successful bead")
601		assert.True(t, sawFailure, "bead.result missing for failed bead")
602	
603		// loop.end must summarise attempts/successes/failures.
604		endData := byType["loop.end"][0]
605		assert.EqualValues(t, 2, endData["attempts"])
606		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, endData["successes"])
607		assert.EqualValues(t, 1, endData["failures"])
608		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusExecutionFailed, endData["last_failure_status"])

796	// TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue verifies that a bead with
797	// repeated no_changes results cannot prevent other ready beads from being
798	// executed across multiple queue passes. It also verifies the full adjudication
799	// lifecycle: other beads run unblocked while the no-changes bead is open, and
800	// the no-changes bead is eventually closed as already_satisfied at the threshold.
801	func TestExecuteBeadWorkerNoChangesDoesNotStarveQueue(t *testing.T) {
802		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
803		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
804	
805		// Three beads: one that always returns no_changes (no cooldown), two that succeed.
806		// Not supplying BaseRev/ResultRev means shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false,
807		// so no retry-after is written and the bead stays immediately retryable between
808		// passes. This keeps the test deterministic without mocking time.
809		ncBead := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-nc10", Title: "Always no-changes", Priority: 0}
810		work1 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk11", Title: "Work bead 1", Priority: 1}
811		work2 := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-wk12", Title: "Work bead 2", Priority: 2}
812		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ncBead))
813		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work1))
814		require.NoError(t, store.Create(work2))
815	
816		var mu sync.Mutex
817		executed := []string{}
818	
819		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
820			Store: store,
821			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
822				mu.Lock()
823				executed = append(executed, beadID)
824				mu.Unlock()
825				if beadID == ncBead.ID {
826					// No BaseRev/ResultRev → shouldSuppressNoProgress returns false
827					// → no cooldown written → bead is immediately retryable.
828					return ExecuteBeadReport{
829						BeadID: beadID,
830						Status: ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges,
831					}, nil
832				}
833				return ExecuteBeadReport{
834					BeadID:    beadID,
835					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
836					SessionID: "sess-" + beadID,
837					ResultRev: "bbbb",
838				}, nil
839			}),
840		}
841	
842		const threshold = 2
843		opts := ExecuteBeadLoopOptions{
844			Assignee:                "worker",
845			MaxNoChangesBeforeClose: threshold,
846		}
847	
848		// Pass 1: all three beads are ready.
849		// ncBead returns no_changes (count=1 < threshold) and stays open.
850		// work1 and work2 succeed and are closed.
851		// The `attempted` map inside Run prevents ncBead from being picked up
852		// a second time within the same pass — the other beads run unblocked.
853		result1, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
854		require.NoError(t, err)
855		assert.Equal(t, 3, result1.Attempts, "all three beads must be attempted in pass 1")
856		assert.Equal(t, 2, result1.Successes)
857		assert.Equal(t, 1, result1.Failures)
858		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, result1.LastFailureStatus)
859	
860		w1, _ := store.Get(work1.ID)
861		w2, _ := store.Get(work2.ID)
862		nc, _ := store.Get(ncBead.ID)
863		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w1.Status)
864		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, w2.Status)
865		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusOpen, nc.Status, "ncBead must stay open after first no_changes")
866	
867		// Pass 2: only ncBead remains; count reaches threshold → closed as already_satisfied.
868		result2, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
869		require.NoError(t, err)
870		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Attempts)
871		assert.Equal(t, 1, result2.Successes, "ncBead must be closed as already_satisfied")
872		require.Len(t, result2.Results, 1)
873		assert.Equal(t, ExecuteBeadStatusAlreadySatisfied, result2.Results[0].Status)
874	
875		nc, _ = store.Get(ncBead.ID)
876		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, nc.Status)
877	
878		// Pass 3: queue is empty.
879		result3, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), opts)
880		require.NoError(t, err)
881		assert.True(t, result3.NoReadyWork)
882		assert.Equal(t, 0, result3.Attempts)
883	
884		// ncBead was attempted exactly twice (once per pass), never a third time.
885		ncExec := 0
886		for _, id := range executed {
887			if id == ncBead.ID {
888				ncExec++
889			}
890		}
891		assert.Equal(t, 2, ncExec, "ncBead must be executed exactly twice across all passes")
892	}

27	// Resolve produces a sealed ResolvedConfig by layering overrides onto
28	// cfg. Overrides are applied as last-write-wins per field. The
29	// returned ResolvedConfig does not alias cfg's storage — every map
30	// and slice is deep-cloned via per-type Clone methods before being
31	// captured.

33	// Resolve accepts a nil cfg and returns a ResolvedConfig populated
34	// from package defaults, sealed normally.
35	func (c *NewConfig) Resolve(overrides CLIOverrides) ResolvedConfig {
36		r := ResolvedConfig{sealed: true}
37	
38		r.assignee = overrides.Assignee
39		r.reviewMaxRetries = c.ResolveReviewMaxRetries()
40	
41		var agent *AgentConfig
42		var workers *WorkersConfig
43		if c != nil {
44			agent = c.Agent.Clone()
45			workers = c.Workers
46		}
47	
48		r.noProgressCooldown = workers.ResolveNoProgressCooldown()
49		r.maxNoChangesBeforeClose = workers.ResolveMaxNoChangesBeforeClose()
50		r.heartbeatInterval = workers.ResolveHeartbeatInterval()
51	
52		r.harness = overrides.Harness
53		if r.harness == "" && agent != nil {
54			r.harness = agent.Harness
55		}
56	
57		r.model = overrides.Model
58		if r.model == "" && agent != nil {
59			r.model = agent.Model
60		}
61	
62		r.provider = overrides.Provider
63		r.modelRef = overrides.ModelRef
64		r.profile = overrides.Profile
65		r.minTier = overrides.MinTier
66		r.maxTier = overrides.MaxTier
67		r.effort = overrides.Effort
68	
69		r.permissions = overrides.Permissions
70		if r.permissions == "" && agent != nil {
71			r.permissions = agent.Permissions
72		}
73	
74		if overrides.Timeout != nil {
75			r.timeout = *overrides.Timeout
76		} else if agent != nil && agent.TimeoutMS > 0 {
77			r.timeout = time.Duration(agent.TimeoutMS) * time.Millisecond
78		}
79	
80		r.evidenceCaps = c.ResolveEvidenceCaps(r.harness)
81	
82		if agent != nil {
83			r.sessionLogDir = agent.SessionLogDir
84			if agent.ReasoningLevels != nil {
85				r.reasoningLevels = make(map[string][]string, len(agent.ReasoningLevels))
86				for k, v := range agent.ReasoningLevels {

88				}

90			if agent.Routing != nil {

93				}
94			}

99			}

102		if c != nil && c.Executions != nil && c.Executions.Mirror != nil {

109	// LoadAndResolve is the canonical production entry point that produces
110	// a sealed ResolvedConfig. It loads the project's .ddx/config.yaml from
111	// projectRoot, layers overrides on top, and returns the immutable
112	// ResolvedConfig.
113	//
114	// On load error, LoadAndResolve still returns a usable, sealed
